description: Fig. A1. Mixed-gender elections around the US. This figure plots the total number of mixed-gender elections normalized by the number of districts in each US state from the 109th to 116th Congress. (For interpretation of the colors in the figure(s), the reader is referred to the web version of this article.)

Fig. A2. Covariate balance test, pool of government contractors. This figure considers variables that describes the pool of government contractors before the election. The figures show the results of a covariate balance test in the mixed elections in our sample. The black dots represent averages for 20 equally sized bins. The solid black lines are predictions based on a third-order polynomial interacted with a female winner indicator variable. The dotted line indicates the 95% confidence interval.

Fig. A3. Covariate balance test, American Community Survey. These figures consider variables describing the demographics of the district, taken from the American Community Survey, which provides yearly data on district level starting in 2010. Otherwise, the figures follow Fig. A2.

Fig. A4. Placebo regressions. This figure displays a histogram of coefficients of 10,000 placebo regressions for the OLS specification in Table 4, Model 3. For each of the 10,000 iterations, we use our main sample of mixed-gender elections with absolute vote margin below 30% and randomize the gender of the winning candidate keeping all other variables constant. The red dashed line indicates the coefficient in Table 4, Model 3.

Fig. A5. Robustness – removing individual states from the sample. This figure shows the cross-sectional distribution of parameter estimates for the local OLS specification in Table 4, Model 3, when excluding one US state at a time from the sample. In the figure, we also report the actual estimated coefficients.
